mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals stronger labor market conditions, increasing corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production indicates stronger real economic activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO rises by >1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly acceleration in GDP growth signals stronger aggregate demand, increasing corporate revenue visibility and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation (CPI) and the federal funds rate signal accommodative monetary policy, reducing real interest rates and boosting equity valuations for S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL < 2.0% yoy and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment growth during a high-employment regime signals sustained labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances during periods when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k/month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in housing starts indicates stronger consumer demand for durable goods, increasing corporate sales visibility and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST rises by >10% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals stronger consumer spending, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS rises by >1.0% month-over-month.
